DECLARATION OF EDWARD W SUMAN IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS (TRANSACTION ID # 56727525) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FOND EXPRESS INTERNATIONAL(SFO), INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS CONTINUED FROM FEB-06-2015 TO MAR-06-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 6, 2015 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). FURTHER, ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS, THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS DOES NOT REFLECT THAT THEY WERE EVER FILED. OPPOSING PARTY MUST FILE HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 6, 2015 AT 4:00 PM IN ORDER FOR THEM TO BE CONSIDERED BY THE COURT IN RULING ON THE MOTION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. EDWARD SUMAN, TEL. NO. (650) 583-3200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER A. DEWYS OF DANFOURA LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 970-8012 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HII FINANCE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is Granted. A plaintiff opposing a motion to quash has the initial burden of demonstrating facts establishing a basis for personal jurisdiction. (Snowney v. Harrah's Entertainment, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1054, 1062.) Plaintiff has not met its initial burden. The only piece of admissible evidence, Exhibit A to the Declaration of Lawrence Lee, does not establish that Defendant had the requisite minimum contacts with California to permit jurisdiction over it. The email is signed by "Hassan Judeh on behalf of ISDA and Co." and does not say anything about Defendant being responsible for ISDA's contracts or debt. Further, the email does not say anything about Defendant controlling or operating ISDA. Defendant's objection to Exhibit A to the Declaration of Lawrence Lee (objection no. 4) is overruled. All of Defendant's other objections (objections nos. 1-3, 5) are sustained. The Request to continue the hearing was Denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to conform with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb
